<<ENTER>>

templates.name: Implement
templates.text:
<<ENTER>>

run: git rebase master

{{skills}}
<<ENTER>>

templates.name: Merge
templates.text:
<<ENTER>>
Execute strictly in order:

- If there are untracked or uncommitted changes, commit them first
- Squash all changes in the branch into a single commit (the commit message must start with the ticket number and ticket title)
- Run git -C <main-worktree> merge --ff-only <branch> to fast-forward master
- If the merge fails because master has moved ahead:
    - Save the current HEAD: git rev-parse HEAD (call this PRE_REBASE)
    - Rebase the branch on master
    - Resolve any conflicts
    - Regression check: run git diff PRE_REBASE HEAD -- it must be empty. If not, the rebase silently dropped or altered changes. Fix before continuing.
    - Make all tests pass
    - Run git -C <main-worktree> merge --ff-only <branch> to fast-forward master again
<<ENTER>>

skills.name: Plan
skills.text: /grill-me help me creating a detailed product-requirement-document.md in the ticket folder. use /to-prd to generate the prd at the end. Do not modify status.json.

conflictResolutionPrompt: You are in a scratch git worktree with a rebase already in progress that has conflicts. Stay in this folder; do not cd elsewhere or touch any other directory. Resolve every conflicted file, then run git rebase --continue, repeating until the rebase completes. Then push your result to its upstream branch (the exact push command is given below). Finally verify with git status that the rebase is finished and your work is pushed. CRITICAL: do not leave the rebase unfinished or any commit unpushed.
